description_long: This is a two-story wooden house with a prominent gable roof covered in red shingles. The front features a large, glassed-in upper section with triangular windows and a covered area supported by wooden posts, as well as a double-door garage on the lower right. The side elevations show a simple, rectangular profile with small, evenly spaced windows and a single side door. The rear of the house is plain, with three small windows and a large expanse of wooden siding, set against a stone or concrete base. The structure is primarily constructed from light-colored wood, with visible paneling and trim details. The overall design is functional and straightforward, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on utility.
description: A two-story wooden house with a red gable roof, large front windows, and a double-door garage. The design is simple, with light wood siding and minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large upper windows, covered entry supported by posts, and double-door garage.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the gable end, a single small window, and the stone or concrete base.
description_view_2: Rear view with three small windows, a plain wooden facade, and a side door.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, with a gable end and stone or concrete base visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large front windows and small side/rear windows
attribute_entryway_design: Covered entry supported by wooden posts, with a double-door garage and large upper windows above
